octopus-vinegar-argiro.name: Octopus Vinegar
instructions: We start by cleaning the octopus.
Wash it very well under running water and let it drain.
In a pot, place the octopus with the hood upwards.
Pour it with olive oil, vinegar and wine.
Add the bay leaf and pepper and let them boil over medium heat.
From the time it gets boiled, about 40 minutes or until it softens (depending on how well it is beaten).
When it gets soft, we put it on a platter.

one-pan-dinner.name: One Pan Dinner
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200°C).

Make the jalapeño poppers: Halve the jalapeños lengthwise and use a spoon to scrape out the seeds and ribs. Fill each jalapeño half with 1 tablespoon of cream cheese. Wrap a halved bacon strip around each stuffed jalapeño half.

To make a tray for the jalapeño poppers, take a piece of aluminum foil and fold in 2 inches of each short side. Dog-ear the corners and fold up to lift the remaining sides. The tray should fill ¼ of the baking sheet. Arrange the jalapeño poppers in the tray.
Make the pizza: Grease the empty half of the baking sheet with nonstick spray.
Unroll the pizza dough and place on the greased half of the pan. Spread the sauce evenly across the dough, then sprinkle with the cheese and top with the pepperoni and sliced jalapeño.

Make the s’mores pie: Unfold the thawed puff pastry on a lightly floured surface. Gently roll out to remove any creases. Cut out a 6x8-inch rectangle, reserving the excess pastry for another use. Using a sharp knife, gently score a ½-inch border all the way around the pastry rectangle, being careful to not cut all the way through. Prick holes in the center of the pastry with a fork. Transfer the pastry to the remaining quarter of the baking sheet.
Spread the chocolate-hazelnut spread evenly over the inner rectangle of the pastry, then top with half of the marshmallows and graham crackers.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg and water. Brush the egg wash over the pastry border.
Transfer the pan to the oven and bake for 20–25 minutes, until the pizza dough is cooked through and bacon is crispy.
Remove the pan from the oven. Garnish the pizza with the basil leaves. Top the s’mores pie with the remaining marshmallows and graham crackers, then drizzle with the melted chocolate.
Enjoy!

one-bowl-salmon-and-bean-salad.name: One-bowl Salmon And Bean Salad

ingredients.prep: halved and juiced, plus 4 tablespoons fresh juice from 1 lemon
diced, fennel stalks and fronds reserved for another use

instructions: {'num': '1', 'step': 'For the Beans: In a large bowl, cover beans by at least 4 inches water and let soak overnight. The next day, drain, transfer beans to a large pot, and cover with at least 2 inches of fresh cold water. Add halved onion, garlic, 1 celery stalk, carrot, and 1 bay leaf to pot. Season with salt. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium-high heat, then lower heat to maintain gentle simmer. Cook until beans are fully tender, about 45 minutes. (Bean cooking time will vary depending on age and exact type of bean; taste every 5 minutes until done.) Set aside to cool.'}

{'num': '2', 'step': 'Meanwhile, combine salmon in a large saucepan with enough cold water to cover. Add 1 stalk celery, remaining 1 bay leaf, and lemon halves with their juice. Heat over medium heat until water registers 77°C on an instant-read thermometer (or until water is at just below a bare simmer, if you don’t have a thermometer), then adjust heat to maintain temperature. Cook until salmon is 115 to 49°C in the center, about 20 minutes. (If you don’t have a thermometer, salmon should flake easily when done.) Drain salmon, discard aromatics, and let cool in refrigerator.'}

{'num': '3', 'step': 'Using your fingers, flake salmon into large pieces. In a large bowl, whisk together remaining 4 tablespoons lemon juice, olive oil, shallot, and dill. Season with salt and pepper. Add cooled and drained beans, diced celery, diced fennel, and arugula and toss well. Add salmon, season with salt and pepper, and toss gently to combine. Serve right away.'}

one-tray-caprese-pasta.name: One-tray Caprese Pasta
instructions: Preheat oven to 400ºF (200ºC).
Take two sheets of aluminum foil (about 12x12-inch, 30x30 cm) and stack them on top of each other.
Fold one side of the foil about ⅓ of the way across sheet, repeat for opposite side.
Pinch the corner to form a point and then flatten it to the short side of the foil, forming a raised corner. Repeat for all four sides, and make four of these.
Add all of the ingredients to one of the foil boats, then stir.
Bake for 12 minutes.
Allow to cool for 10 minutes.
Eat or pack into tupperware and refrigerate 3-5 days.
Enjoy!
